entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The target crop of the review, noted for being a highly monomorphic species with a narrow genetic base

entities.name: POLYPLOIDATION
entities.description: The process of genome duplication in peanut that contributed to its monomorphic nature

entities.name: GEOGRAPHICAL ISOLATION
entities.description: A factor that, along with polyploidation, rendered peanut a monomorphic species

entities.name: MONOMORPHIC SPECIES
entities.description: A species with very little genetic variation, describing the current state of peanut

entities.name: NARROW GENETIC BASE
entities.description: The limited genetic diversity found in cultivated peanut, making it susceptible to diseases

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions that cause economic loss to peanut farmers

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut to resist diseases, which is limited in cultivated varieties

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING PRACTICES
entities.description: Traditional methods used to improve peanut, which have seen limited success due to few resistance sources

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: Tools used to identify disease resistance genomic regions and assist in breeding

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE GENOMIC REGIONS
entities.description: Specific areas of the peanut genome associated with the trait of disease resistance

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Data and tools used to identify molecular markers and improve peanut diversity

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Advanced technologies used to identify a large number of molecular markers

entities.name: PEANUT DIVERSITY
entities.description: The genetic variation within peanut, which molecular markers help to improve

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of improving peanut traits, assisted by molecular markers
